mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market and overall economic health, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investor confidence rises.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, falling unemployment correlates with economic expansion.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with a declining UNRATE over a 3-year rolling window, or if the average return is negative despite falling unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation (CPIAUCSL annual growth) and the federal funds rate are low, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment, reducing the cost of capital and supporting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Low interest rates and stable prices generally support equity markets.
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.2% and CPIAUCSL annual growth is below 2%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average or show no statistically significant outperformance compared to other regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Robust consumer demand, evidenced by strong retail sales and increasing housing starts, indicates a healthy economic expansion that translates into higher corporate revenues and S&P 500 appreciation over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations improve.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending and housing are key drivers of economic growth.
failure_condition: If, when RSAFS 3-month % change is > 1% and HOUST 3-month % change is > 5%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During economic recovery phases, an acceleration in industrial production growth signals increasing manufacturing activity and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the economy normalizes.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production rebound is typical in post-recession recoveries.
failure_condition: If, in identified post-recession recovery periods, INDPRO growth exceeding 0.5% over 3 months does not lead to positive S&P 500 60-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in non-farm payrolls growth combined with a rising unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and an impending economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days due to reduced corporate outlooks.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth < 0.1% AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Weakening labor markets precede economic contractions.
failure_condition: If, when PAYEMS 3-month average growth is below 0.1% and UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points in 3 months, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real GDP reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which generally supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 performance over the medium term (60 trading days) as investor confidence remains high.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a fundamental indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 year-over-year growth over any 5-year rolling window, or if average returns are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate, indicating aggressive monetary tightening, raises borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, negatively impacting corporate profitability and S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days due to increased discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:Rapid rate hikes typically precede market corrections.
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.5% over 3 months, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a significant decline in real retail sales indicates weakening consumer purchasing power and demand, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month % change < -0.5% AND CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation erodes real purchasing power, hurting retail sales.
failure_condition: If, when RSAFS 3-month % change is below -0.5% and CPIAUCSL annual growth is above 3%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such occurrences.
